description: Links between extracted PDF photos and places from the letter collection

ravnholt.description: Trine Gad's family home (Ravnholt), near Roager. Not yet in places.geojson — candidate for addition

Russian prisoners at Ravnholt 1915 with Trine

laon.description: Western front, Peter stationed here 1917. Famous cathedral

cessieres.description: Western front village, Peter stationed autumn 1917

viviase.description: Western front, Peter at Staben here Dec 1917. Musse born while Peter was here

photos.description: Julebordet i Viviase for Staben, Dec 24 1917

grandlup.description: Western front, Peter and Uffe (svoger) met here 1918

photos.description: Grandlup La Neuville, Uffe and Peter together

kongeaaen.description: Kongeåen — the Danish-German border 1864-1920. Peter's home was just south of it

photos.description: Historical map of Kongeåen border near Ribe

villa_vinterhistorie.country: Latvia (then Russian Empire, occupied by Germany)
villa_vinterhistorie.description: Soldier-built jordhytte/bunker at Boronikischski, west of Dünaburg (Daugavpils). Peter and Konow lived here winter 1915-16. Also known as 'Willa Fred' (Oct 1915), 'Villa Vinterhistorie' (Nov 1915-Jan 1916), and 'Villa Wintermärchen' (German name, Mar 1916). Peter's own later note: 'En hel Vinter, selvbygget'. Description from letter 17 Nov 1915: 'Jordhytten består af et Rum på 18 m i omfang, 1,80 høj, til højre 3 Sengesteder over hinanden, til venstre et bord, afsavede tykke Granstammer tjæner som Stole, et Vindue sammensat af mindst 100 stykker glas, men kun tre Ruder.'

photos.description: Interior of Villa Vinterhistorie: Peter, Konow and comrades by candlelight, playing cards at improvised table
Jordhytte with birch fence (Hegn af hvide Birkestammer)
